display.purpose: Home hero name only; capped under 6rem.

headline.purpose: Page h1 and major section heads.

code.purpose: Inline code and code blocks — the hero content.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Feature cards and dropdowns on hover/open.

motion.name: ease-state
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for color/state transitions, ~0.2s. Crossfade fallback under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Brand Button
components.description: The single primary action per view (e.g. Get started).
components.css: .ds-btn-brand { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 40px; padding: 0 20px; background: #6d28d9; color: #fff; font: 500 0.8125rem/1 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; border: none; border-radius: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-brand:hover { background: #7c3aed; } .ds-btn-brand:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6d28d9; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Alt Button
components.css: .ds-btn-alt { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 40px; padding: 0 20px; background: #f6f6f7; color: #3c3c43; font: 500 0.8125rem/1 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; border: none; border-radius: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-alt:hover { background: #e2e2e3; } .ds-btn-alt:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6d28d9; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Landing-page feature box; flat at rest, lifts on hover. Never nested.
components.html:
One API, two modes

A StoreValue is a plain in-memory value until it isn't. Bind a doc and the same handle becomes a CRDT.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #f6f6f7; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; transition: box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 3px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.07), 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.07); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font: 600 1.125rem/1.4 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #3c3c43; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font: 400 0.9375rem/1.6 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: #67676c; }

components.name: Code Block
components.description: The hero content. Quiet surface so syntax highlighting carries the meaning.
components.html:
const counter = new StoreValue(0)
counter.set(1) // logs 1
components.css: .ds-code { margin: 0; background: #f6f6f7; border: 1px solid #e2e2e3; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 24px; overflow-x: auto; font: 400 0.875em/1.7 ui-monospace, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; color: #3c3c43; } .ds-code .ds-cmt { color: #98989f; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Top-nav link; active item is the only Signal Violet on the bar.
components.html: GuideReferenceExamples
components.css: .ds-nav { display: inline-flex; gap: 24px; } .ds-nav-item { font: 500 0.8125rem/1.4 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: #67676c; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { color: #3c3c43; } .ds-nav-item.ds-active { color: #6d28d9; } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6d28d9; outline-offset: 4px; border-radius: 2px; }

narrative.overview: The super-store docs read like a precise tool that hides its effort. The library's pitch is that a StoreValue looks like plain in-memory state until you bind a document — and the design honors that exact restraint. The surface is calm, near-monochrome, and code-forward; the one violet accent does the signalling and nothing else carries decoration. Power is felt in the legibility of a snippet and the exactness of a guarantee, never announced with gradients, glows, or motion spectacle. This is a brand surface: the public face of an OSS library, where a skeptical engineer decides in seconds whether to trust it. The design's job is trust through precision.
keyCharacteristics: Code is the hero; chrome recedes.
One violet accent, used sparingly and deliberately.
Near-monochrome neutral surface, AA-legible in both light and dark.
Flat by default; depth appears only as a response to interaction.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Violet appears on ≤10% of any screen — links, one CTA, the active nav item. Its rarity is what makes it read as signal.

rules.name: The Gradient-Is-Sacred Rule
rules.body: The violet→blue gradient exists only on the hero name. Gradient text anywhere else is prohibited.

rules.name: The Single-Family Rule
rules.body: One sans (Inter) plus one mono. Contrast comes from weight and scale, never from a third font.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow appears only as feedback to hover, focus, or elevation.

dos: Do make code the hero — give snippets room, strong syntax legibility, and never let chrome compete.
Do keep Signal Violet to ≤10% of any screen (links, one CTA, active nav).
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; reveal a shadow only on hover/focus/open.
Do hold body text at 65–75ch and ≥4.5:1 contrast in both themes; muted text still clears AA.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce with a crossfade or instant fallback on every transition.
donts: Don't ship a generic VitePress default — a swapped accent over the stock theme is not an identity.
Don't drift into crypto/web3 maximalism: no neon glows, heavy glassmorphism, animated noise, or dark-only spectacle.
Don't clutter — no badge soup, no competing accents, no side-stripe borders, no decorative noise.
Don't use gradient text anywhere but the home hero name.
Don't introduce a second accent hue or a third font.
Don't nest cards or add a resting drop shadow to a card.
